verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal dual lipid modifications (myristoylation at Gly2 and palmitoylation at Cys3). Its kinase domain and SH2/SH3 domains all face the cytoplasm. No extracellular domain or ectodomain exists. Checking each contextual bucket: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented stress- or activation-induced translocation to the extracellular face; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no lineage-specific outer-leaflet display reported; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — inner-leaflet PM pool is well documented but no outer-leaflet pool is known; (5) stable_surface_attachment — not secreted, not shed, no extracellular domain to anchor. No therapeutic program targets LYN from the extracellular side. All documented LYN inhibitors are cytoplasmic-penetrating small molecules (e.g., dasatinib). Inner-leaflet lipid anchor places it firmly in the inner_leaflet_anchored category.
